title: When the Lights Come On
synopsis: Marcus must navigate a magical traveling carnival alone for the first time to solve a mystery about missing carousel animals, guided by observations from a clever crow who sees what humans overlook.

pages.text: The carousel horses gallop away when no one is watching. I know because I am a crow, and crows see everything. Tonight, Marcus stands at the carnival ticket booth with his map clutched tight. His grandmother said he could explore alone this time - a first for someone who just turned six. The ticket seller has fingers that shimmer like water, and when she hands Marcus his purple wristband, tiny stars fall from her sleeves onto the ground.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes receives a glowing purple wristband from a shimmering ticket seller. A black crow perches on the booth watching. Warm yellows and oranges glow from carnival lights. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus getting his wristband at the magical ticket booth

pages.text: Marcus studies his map carefully. Three locations are marked in gold ink: the Carousel Garden, the House of Mirrors, and the Candy Cloud Kitchen. But as I watch from my perch on the ticket booth, I notice something Marcus cannot see. The carousel animals on his map keep moving, rearranging themselves in different positions every few seconds. A painted horse switches places with a tiger. A dragon trades spots with a swan. They are trying to tell him something important.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes holds a magical map with moving illustrations. A black crow observes from above. The map glows with warm yellows and oranges showing three carnival locations. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus studying his magical moving map

pages.text: "The animals are worried," I whisper to the wind, but Marcus speaks only human language. He folds the map and heads toward the Carousel Garden, his sneakers crunching on star-dust that fell from the ticket seller's sleeves. The carnival music plays backwards tonight, which means something magical has gone wrong. Marcus doesn't know this yet, but he will figure it out. Children who carry their own maps always do.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes walks away from the ticket booth toward glowing carnival lights, leaving glittering footprints. A black crow flies overhead. Warm yellows and oranges illuminate the path. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus walking into the carnival leaving magical footprints

pages.text: The carousel spins with empty saddles. Every carved animal has vanished except for one small wooden rabbit who trembles on his golden pole. Marcus circles the moving platform three times, counting the vacant spaces. Twenty-seven animals should live here, but I count only hoofprints in sawdust and the lonely rabbit who shivers despite the warm carnival air. The carousel operator, a woman with peacock feathers for hair, shrugs when Marcus asks where everyone went.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes stands beside a nearly empty carousel with one small wooden rabbit. A woman with peacock feather hair operates the ride. A black crow perches on the carousel roof. Warm yellows and oranges light the scene. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus at the nearly empty carousel with the operator

pages.text: "They wander off sometimes," she says, but her peacock feathers droop sadly. Marcus kneels down and finds tiny hoofprints leading away from the carousel. The prints glow silver in the carnival light - too small for real horses, too perfect for anything but magic. I hop closer and see what Marcus misses: the hoofprints spell out words in a language older than human speech. They say "House of Mirrors" in the ancient tongue of painted things.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes kneels examining glowing silver hoofprints on the ground. A black crow stands nearby pointing with its wing. Warm yellows and oranges illuminate the magical tracks. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus discovering magical hoofprints with the crow's help

pages.text: The wooden rabbit speaks in a voice like wind chimes: "They went looking for their reflections. Carousel animals need to see themselves to remember who they are." Marcus nods seriously, the way children do when magic makes perfect sense. He checks his map again, and I watch as the House of Mirrors begins pulsing with warm light on the paper. The rabbit adds, "But mirrors can be tricky for painted animals. Sometimes they forget which side is real."

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes listens to a wooden rabbit on the carousel. The rabbit glows softly as it speaks. A black crow watches the conversation. Warm yellows and oranges create a magical atmosphere. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: The wooden rabbit speaking to Marcus about the missing animals

pages.text: Inside the House of Mirrors, everything reflects except the carnival animals. Marcus sees hundreds of himself - tall, short, wide, thin - but where twenty-six painted creatures should cast reflections, the mirrors show only empty spaces shaped like horses and tigers and dragons. I perch on his shoulder because crows appear in mirrors just fine. We are real in a way that painted animals struggle to be.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes stands in a mirror maze with multiple reflections. A black crow sits on his shoulder. Empty animal-shaped spaces float in some mirrors. Warm yellows and oranges reflect infinitely. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus in the House of Mirrors with animal-shaped empty spaces

pages.text: "We're stuck," whispers a painted horse from somewhere behind the glass. Marcus presses his hands against mirror after mirror, searching for his new friends. I tap my beak against one reflection and hear the hollow sound that means a secret door. Marcus pushes, and the mirror swings open like a window. Beyond it, all twenty-six carousel animals crowd together in a space between reflections, looking confused and faded around the edges.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes pushes open a mirror door. Behind it, faded carousel animals huddle together looking lost. A black crow helps guide Marcus to the secret opening. Warm yellows and oranges light the hidden space. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus opening the secret mirror door to find the lost animals

pages.text: "Mirrors confused us," explains a painted dragon with silver scales. "We forgot which way leads home." Marcus understands immediately. He takes out his map and holds it up so all the animals can see their pictures moving on the paper. "Follow your map-selves," he says with the confidence of someone handling something important completely alone for the first time. "They remember the way back." One by one, the animals step through the mirror-door, growing brighter as they move toward home.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes holds up his glowing map to show confused carousel animals. The animals look hopeful as they see their moving pictures. A black crow watches proudly. Warm yellows and oranges illuminate the rescue scene. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus showing the map to help the lost carousel animals

pages.text: Back at the Carousel Garden, each animal finds their golden pole and settles into place. The painted horse nods his thanks. The dragon winks one silver eye. The wooden rabbit bounces with joy as his friends return. Marcus climbs onto the rabbit for his first ride, and as the carousel spins, I see something wonderful: his reflection in the mirror panels now includes all twenty-seven painted friends, as if solving their mystery made him part of their magical family forever.

illustration.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes rides the wooden rabbit on the now-full carousel. All the painted animals are back in their places. His reflection shows him surrounded by all his new animal friends. A black crow flies in celebration. Warm yellows and oranges create a joyful scene. Cozy kitchen home art style.
illustration.alt: Marcus riding the carousel with all animals returned and reflecting together

morals: Sometimes helping others helps us find where we belong
Being brave when alone can lead to wonderful friendships

coverPrompt.description: A boy with short curly hair with a fade, medium-dark brown skin, and warm brown eyes stands at the entrance of a glowing carnival at dusk, with a black crow perched nearby. Warm yellows and oranges light up carnival tents and rides. Cozy kitchen home art style with warm colors.
coverPrompt.alt: Marcus at carnival entrance with crow guide

questions.question: What makes Marcus special at the carnival?
choices: He can fly like the crow
He's exploring completely alone for the first time
questions.explanation: This is Marcus's first time handling something important completely alone, which makes him brave and special.

questions.question: Why were the carousel animals missing?
choices: They were looking for their reflections
questions.explanation: The wooden rabbit explains that carousel animals went looking for their reflections to remember who they are.

questions.question: What was wrong with the mirrors?
choices: The animals couldn't see their reflections
questions.explanation: The mirrors showed empty animal-shaped spaces where the painted creatures should have cast reflections.

questions.question: How did Marcus help the lost animals?
choices: He showed them their moving pictures on his map
questions.explanation: Marcus held up his map so the animals could see their pictures moving on the paper, helping them remember the way home.

questions.question: Who tells this story?
questions.explanation: The story is told by a crow who notices things humans miss and watches Marcus's adventure unfold.
